figures.caption: FIG. 1. Finite-size scaling of the Binder cumulant USG for the gauge-correlated Nishimori spin glass in two dimensions. (a) Standard Edwards-Anderson scaling (du = 6) with ν = 2.5 fails catastrophically for macroscopic system sizes, exhibiting a large variance penalty (O(10−3)). (b) Pure logarithmic scaling (du → 0) shows significantly improved collapse, but retains a systematic finite-size drift due to the slow decay of the marginal cubic operator. (c) The fully corrected topological data collapse utilizing the multiplicative continuum metric factor L0 ≈ 0.94. By evaluating strictly in the macroscopic continuum regime (L ∈ [64, 1024]), the variance drops to an absolute minimum (O(10−4)), perfectly validating the BKT-like marginal fixed point prediction without disturbing the saturated order parameter in the low-temperature phase.
